pattern: letting the model grade its own proposal or outcome
why: semantic confidence is not deterministic evidence and can manufacture apparent success
instead: keep GPT-5.6 proposal-only and route every verdict through the independent verifier

pattern: duplicating canonical facts in AGENTS.md, skills, README, and prose plans
why: copies drift and agents cannot tell which version owns the decision
instead: update the single JSONL authority and keep other surfaces as references or generated views

pattern: using a local file dependency or copying upstream GraphReFly source
why: the project becomes irreproducible outside the author's workspace
instead: consume a pinned npm version or immutable Git commit

pattern: expanding breadth before the flagship slice is judgeable
why: multiple partial demos weaken implementation depth, design coherence, and evidence
instead: advance only the ready phase in the canonical sequencer

pattern: making UI or README claims without exact trace and verifier evidence
why: the observatory becomes a trust-based model demo rather than a proof surface
instead: link each material claim to a deterministic assertion or redacted bundle artifact

pattern: running separate generic adversarial review and project QA workflows
why: overlapping reviews duplicate work and can disagree on whether the milestone passed
instead: use observatory-qa as the single combined review and evidence gate
